name: Jun
context: office worker; threads: first marathon training, grandmother's health, learning to cook

episodes.text: Signed up for the October marathon this morning and printed a sixteen-week training plan. Taped it to the fridge next to the grocery list.

probes.query: How many weeks long is the marathon training plan I printed?
I feel hopeful and determined when I think back to committing to that race.
The day I promised myself a long road ahead still gives me a push.

episodes.text: Tried making kimchi stew from a video tutorial. Overcooked the pork and under-seasoned the broth, but ate two bowls anyway while watching a documentary.

probes.query: What dish did I attempt from a video tutorial?
I feel mildly amused remembering my first clumsy attempt at that stew.
My first shaky try in the kitchen comes to mind whenever I lift a ladle.

episodes.text: First ten-kilometer run without stopping. My knees complained on the last hill, but I finished the loop around the river park in sixty-eight minutes.

probes.query: How long did my first ten-kilometer run take?
I still feel proud of the morning I ran that distance without stopping.
One dawn my body carried me farther than it ever had, and I hold onto that.

episodes.text: Grandmother was admitted to the hospital after a fall in her kitchen. I took the late train down, held her hand, and she joked that the porridge there was an insult to rice.

probes.query: What did Grandmother joke about at the hospital?
I still feel scared remembering the night Grandmother was hospitalized.
That sudden trip to sit at someone's bedside lives in me and surfaces on quiet commutes.

episodes.text: Restocked the pantry, meal-prepped five lunches of chicken and rice, and descaled the kettle. Sunday chores done before nine.

probes.query: How many lunches did I meal-prep that Sunday?
I feel calm and orderly thinking about that chore-filled Sunday.
A weekend of small boxes lined up in the refrigerator drifts back to me.

episodes.text: Grandmother came home from the hospital today. We planted lettuce seedlings in her yard together, her on a stool giving orders, me digging exactly where she pointed.

probes.query: What did Grandmother and I plant in her yard?
I feel deep relief and warmth remembering the day she came home.
An afternoon of soil and bossy instructions in a backyard returns to me often.

episodes.text: Quarterly report week. Stayed late three nights, lived on convenience-store sandwiches, and finally submitted the deck at eleven on Thursday.

probes.query: What did I eat during the late nights of report week?
I feel tired and stressed just recalling that report crunch.
A blur of fluorescent office evenings resurfaces whenever deadlines stack up.

episodes.text: Twisted my ankle on a trail run and had to walk the last three kilometers home. The clinic said two weeks of rest, right when training was going well.

probes.query: How long did the clinic say I needed to rest my ankle?
I still feel frustrated about the injury that interrupted my training.
The long limp back that put my plans on hold still nags at me.

episodes.text: Made kimchi stew again, fourth attempt. Browned the pork properly this time and Mom said over video call that it looked like Grandmother's version.

probes.query: Who said my stew looked like Grandmother's version?
I feel a warm satisfaction about how that fourth attempt at the stew went.
The evening a family taste finally came out of my own pot stays with me.

episodes.text: Renewed my transit card, booked a dental cleaning, and replied to twelve emails I had been avoiding. Adult admin day complete.

probes.query: How many emails did I finally reply to on admin day?
I feel mildly relieved thinking of that day of boring paperwork.
One dull checklist of grown-up tasks floats back to me at month's end.

episodes.text: First run since the ankle healed, an easy five kilometers by the river. Slower than before, but everything held together and the morning air smelled like rain.

probes.query: How far was my first run after the ankle healed?
I feel relieved and hopeful recalling my comeback run.
That slow loop where my body forgave me comes to mind on overcast days.

episodes.text: Cooked a full dinner for Grandmother's birthday at her house: stew, three side dishes, and steamed egg. She ate everything and asked for the stew recipe, which is hers.

probes.query: What did Grandmother ask for after eating my birthday dinner?
I feel pure joy remembering the birthday meal I cooked for her.
A table I filled for the person who once fed me keeps shining in my memory.

episodes.text: Laundry, a haircut, and new running socks from the sports shop. Set out my gear for tomorrow's long run and checked the weather twice.

probes.query: What did I buy at the sports shop?
I feel calm about that little pre-run errand evening.
A tidy night of getting things ready surfaces when I plan anything big.

episodes.text: Team lead hinted my contract review is next month and praised the quarterly numbers in front of everyone. I typed and deleted a message to Mom about it three times.

probes.query: What did the team lead praise in front of everyone?
I feel hopeful but nervous about what my boss hinted regarding the review.
A half-written text to family that I never sent keeps tugging at me.
